Result: Is there a significant difference in mean height among students with different levels of milk drinking frequency?

H₀: μ₁ = μ₂ = μ₃
H₁: At least one group mean is different

μ₁ = No milk drinking
μ₂ = Occasional milk drinking
μ₃ = Frequent milk drinking
There is sufficient statistical evidence at the 0.05 significance level to conclude that mean height differs across the three milk drinking groups.

Mean Difference of 0 (No milk drinking) vs 1 (Occasional milk drinking): 0.0104
Mean Difference of 0 (No milk drinking) vs 2 (Frequent milk drinking): 0.0364
Mean Difference of 1 (Occasional milk drinking) vs 2 (Frequent milk drinking): 0.0259

ANOVA showed significant differences in mean height among groups, but it does not indicate which group has the highest mean. Tukey HSD post-hoc comparisons identified frequent milk drinkers as the highest, followed by occasional and non-drinkers.
